title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure IAM policy changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to IAM policies will help ensure authentication and authorization controls remain intact.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" Internal Only - General o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log-group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{($.eventName=DeleteGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteUserPolicy)||($.eventName=PutGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=PutRolePolicy)||($.eventName=PutUserPolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicy)||($.eventName=DeletePolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=DeletePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=AttachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DetachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=AttachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=AttachGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachGroupPolicy)}" 4. Note the <iam-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <iam-change-metric> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<iam-changes-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: Internal Only - General aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for IAM policy changes and the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name `<trail-log-group-name>` --filter-name `<iam-changes-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<iam-changes-metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{($.eventName=DeleteGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DeleteUserPolicy)||($.eventName=PutGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=PutRolePolicy)||($.eventName=PutUserPolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicy)||($.eventName=DeletePolicy)||($.eventName=CreatePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=DeletePolicyVersion)||($.eventName=AttachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=DetachRolePolicy)||($.eventName=AttachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachUserPolicy)||($.eventName=AttachGroupPolicy)||($.eventName=DetachGroupPolicy)}' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. Internal Only - General 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<iam-changes-alarm>` --metric-name `<iam-changes-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: Monitoring these changes may result in a number of "false positives," especially in larger environments. This alert may require more tuning than others to eliminate some of those erroneous notifications.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_iam_policy_changes_monitored

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use AWS CLI commands or boto3 methods to execute the following checks:
   - **List CloudTrail Trails:** Use `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` or `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`.
   - **Check Multi-Region Status:** Filter for trails where `IsMultiRegionTrail` is `true`.
   - **Get Trail Status:** Use `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>` or `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name='<trail-name>')` to ensure `IsLogging` is `true`.
   - **Check Event Selectors:** Use `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>` or `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName='<trail-name>')` to verify if `IncludeManagementEvents` is `true` and `ReadWriteType` is set to `All`.
   - **List Metric Filters:** After identifying the `CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn` from the trail, use `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>` or `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName='<trail-log-group-name>')` to confirm metric filters exist with the specified `filterPattern`.
   - **Check CloudWatch Alarms:** Use `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<iam-changes-metric>]'` or `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms(MetricName='<iam-changes-metric>')` to find if an alarm is configured for the changes.
   - **Verify SNS Topic Subscriptions:** Use `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` or `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn='<sns-topic-arn>')` to check for active subscriber(s).

2. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If there is an active multi-region CloudTrail trail successfully logging events, metric filters for IAM policy changes are configured, relevant CloudWatch alarms exist, and there is at least one active subscription to the SNS topic.
   - **Fail:** If any of the checks return no results or configurations do not match expected values (e.g., trail not active, missing metric filters, or alarms).

3. **Example for Metric Filter Verification:**
   - Use `boto3` to list the metric filters and check if the `filterPattern` includes the IAM change events specified in the guidelines. Example expected output must include those specific events indicating active monitoring.

This structured automated approach ensures continuous compliance and monitoring of IAM policy changes as specified in the control.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. The described checks can be performed using AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI, which aligns with the automated nature of the task.
- AWS SDK capabilities, CLI commands, and services like CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS are appropriately leveraged for this compliance check.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct, and the boto3 methods and CLI commands are valid for the described checks.
- The steps cover essential aspects of monitoring IAM policy changes, including checking CloudTrail configuration, metric filters, and CloudWatch alarms.
- The program name follows a reasonable convention but could be more descriptive regarding the specific security check.
- A minor improvement could be specifying the exact filter pattern for IAM policy changes in the example, ensuring clarity on what events are being monitored.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:** The current name, `aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_iam_policy_changes_monitored`, is somewhat descriptive but could be improved to `aws_cloudtrail_iam_policy_change_monitoring` for clarity and conciseness.
- **Filter Pattern Specification:** Include an example of a filter pattern that captures IAM policy changes, such as `"{ ($.eventName = 'PutUserPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'PutGroupPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'PutRolePolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DeleteUserPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DeleteGroupPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DeleteRolePolicy') || ($.eventName = 'CreatePolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DeletePolicy') || ($.eventName = 'CreatePolicyVersion') || ($.eventName = 'DeletePolicyVersion') || ($.eventName = 'AttachRolePolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DetachRolePolicy') || ($.eventName = 'AttachUserPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DetachUserPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'AttachGroupPolicy') || ($.eventName = 'DetachGroupPolicy') }"`.
- **Consideration for AWS Config:** Consider using AWS Config rules to complement this check, as AWS Config can continuously evaluate the configuration of AWS resources against desired configurations.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and aligns with AWS best practices for monitoring IAM policy changes. The use of AWS services and APIs is appropriate and technically sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Modify the program name to `aws_cloudtrail_iam_policy_change_monitoring` for better clarity.
- Include a specific example of a filter pattern for IAM policy changes to enhance the technical accuracy and completeness of the assessment.
- Consider mentioning AWS Config as an additional tool for continuous compliance monitoring.
